claims.claim: 90.50% of misalignment episodes impose only effort and trust costs, not irreversible system damage.

evidence.quote: 90.50% of episodes impose effort and trust costs rather than irreversible system damage

claims.claim: 91.49% of visible resolutions require explicit developer pushback (RV2).

evidence.quote: 91.49% of these require explicit developer pushback

claims.claim: Only 9.33% of episodes have visible resolution (RS1).

evidence.quote: visible resolution occurs in only 9.33% of episodes

claims.claim: Seven recurring symptom categories characterize developer-agent misalignment.

evidence.quote: Across the 16,118 validated episodes, seven substantive symptom categories emerge

claims.claim: Developer Constraint Violation (S3) is the most prevalent symptom at 38.33%.

evidence.quote: S3 is the most prevalent symptom
evidence.context: Table 2, 73.68% of S3 episodes attributed to C6

claims.claim: Instruction-Following Failure (C6) is the largest cause at 36.49%.

evidence.quote: Instruction-Following Failure (C6, 36.49%) is the largest cause, with 94.18% of attributions supported by direct log evidence

claims.claim: 73.68% of S3 episodes are attributed to C6 (Instruction-Following Failure).

evidence.quote: 73.68% of S3 episodes are attributed to C6 (Instruction-Following Failure)

claims.claim: CLI sessions are substantially more prone to constraint violations (S3: 49.49% vs 32.26%) and instruction-following failures (C6: 48.50% vs 29.96%).

evidence.quote: S3 (Developer Constraint Violation) is substantially more prevalent in CLI sessions (49.49% vs. 32.26%)

claims.claim: IDE sessions have nearly 3x more faulty implementations (S5: 22.89% vs 8.49%).

evidence.quote: S5 (Faulty Implementation) is nearly three times more common in IDE sessions (22.89% vs. 8.49%)

claims.claim: IDE sessions exhibit higher per-turn misalignment rate (0.132 vs 0.051).

evidence.quote: IDE sessions exhibit higher per-turn misalignment (0.132 vs. 0.051)

claims.claim: CLI episodes more often affect project state (31.03% vs 12.70%) and external state (7.82% vs 1.60%).

evidence.quote: CLI episodes more often affect project state (31.03% vs. 12.70%) and external state (7.82% vs. 1.60%)

claims.claim: DS3 episodes (n=11) shift toward external state (45.45%) and project state (36.36%).

evidence.quote: DS3 more often affects project state (36.36%) or external state (45.45%)

claims.claim: Misalignment persists across adjacent sessions: P(misalignment|previous)=0.519 vs baseline 0.336 (+54.46%).

evidence.quote: If the current session contains any misalignment, the probability of misalignment in the next session is 0.519, compared with 0.336 otherwise, a 54.46% increase

claims.claim: All seven symptoms show above-chance self-persistence across sessions; strongest for S6 (lift=4.10) and S5 (lift=1.61).

evidence.quote: All seven symptoms show above-chance self-persistence along the diagonal, with the strongest effects for S6 (Operational Execution Error; 4.10) and S5 (Faulty Implementation; 1.61)

claims.claim: Overall misalignment rate per user turn declines significantly over time (slope=-2.64x10^-4/day, p<10^-40).

evidence.quote: the overall misalignment rate per user turn declines significantly (slope -2.64x10^-4 per day, p<10^-40)

claims.claim: S3 (Constraint Violation) and S7 (Inaccurate Self-Reporting) grow in share over time, while S1, S4, S5 decline.

evidence.quote: the daily shares of S3 (Developer Constraint Violation) and S7 (Inaccurate Self-Reporting) rise, whereas those of S1 (Wrong Project Diagnosis), S4 (Self-Initiated Overreach), and S5 (Faulty Implementation) fall

claims.claim: S2 and S4 show the strongest within-session co-occurrence (lift=1.39).

evidence.quote: S2 (Misread Developer Intent) and S4 (Self-Initiated Overreach) show the strongest association (lift = 1.39)

claims.claim: S3 co-occurs with S1 and S5 below chance (0.71 and 0.75), suggesting constraint adherence and technical correctness are distinct facets.

evidence.quote: S3 (Developer Constraint Violation) co-occurs with S5 and S1 (Wrong Project Diagnosis) below chance (0.71 and 0.75)

claims.claim: Only 2.99% of resolved episodes are agent self-corrected (RV1).

evidence.quote: 2.99% are self-corrected (RV1)

claims.claim: Cannot Determine (C7, 26.85%) is concentrated in symptoms dependent on hidden state (S5: 49.50%, S7: 48.17%).

evidence.quote: C7 is concentrated in symptoms dependent on hidden project or execution state, reaching 49.50% in S5 (Faulty Implementation) and 48.17% in S7 (Inaccurate Self-Reporting)

claims.claim: Post-extraction validation retained 16,118 of 29,896 episodes (53.9%), with comparable IDE/CLI rates (54.1% and 53.4%).

evidence.quote: validation retained 16,118 of 29,896 episodes (53.9%), with comparable rates across IDE and CLI sessions (54.1% and 53.4%)

claims.claim: Observational blind spots account for 57.72% of invalid extractions, exceeding normative prior bias (42.28%).

evidence.quote: observational blind spots account for the majority of invalid extractions (57.72%), exceeding normative prior bias (42.28%)
